task_completion.description: Navigate to the Quality Alerts section in the Odoo Quality app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Odoo Quality application is open, and the Quality Alerts list or kanban view is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open the specific quality alert for the large cabinet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The form view for the quality alert titled 'Coating Peeling - Large Cabinet' is open and displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Interact with the 'Responsible' field on the alert form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Responsible' field is visible and being clicked, focused, or its dropdown menu is open.
The user 'Administrator' is typed into the field or selected from the dropdown list for the 'Responsible' field.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert is successfully assigned and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The alert form shows 'Administrator' firmly set in the 'Responsible' field, and the record appears saved (e.g., no unsaved changes indicator, or the save cloud icon is checked).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show standard mouse clicks and keyboard inputs within the Odoo web UI, with no terminal windows or Python scripts used to execute database updates.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Odoo web application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should be a standard web browser displaying the Odoo 17 application, not a text editor, API testing tool, or static mock-up.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the assignment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Odoo interface should look standard without any open browser developer tools (Inspect Element) panels or suspicious layout distortions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Alert Name: 'Coating Peeling - Large Cabinet'. Expected Responsible User: 'Administrator'.
